2604.09037.title: SiMing-Bench: Evaluating Procedural Correctness from Continuous Interactions in Clinical Skill Videos
2604.09037.paper_summary: Current video benchmarks for multimodal large language models (MLLMs) focus on event recognition, temporal ordering, and long-context recall, but overlook a harder capability required for expert procedural judgment: tracking how ongoing interactions update the procedural state and thereby determine the correctness of later actions. We introduce SiMing-Bench, the first benchmark for evaluating this capability from full-length clinical skill videos. It targets rubric-grounded process-level judgment of whether interaction-driven state updates preserve procedural correctness across an entire workflow. SiMing-Bench is instantiated with SiMing-Score, a physician-annotated dataset of real clinical skill examination videos spanning cardiopulmonary resuscitation, automated external defibrillator operation, and bag-mask ventilation, each paired with a standardized step-wise rubric and dual-expert labels. Across diverse open- and closed-source MLLMs, we observe consistently weak agreement with physician judgments. Moreover, weak performance on rubric-defined intermediate steps persists even when overall procedure-level correlation appears acceptable, suggesting that coarse global assessment substantially overestimates current models' procedural judgment ability. Additional analyses with binary step judgment and step-aligned clips indicate that the bottleneck is not merely fine-grained scoring or temporal localization, but modeling how continuous interactions update procedural state over time.
2604.09037.paper_summary_zh: 目前針對多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）的視頻基準測試專注於事件識別、時間排序和長期記憶，但忽略了專業程序判斷所需的一項更難的能力：追蹤持續互動如何更新程序狀態，並因此決定後續行動的正確性。我們介紹了SiMing-Bench，這是第一個用於評估此能力的基準，基於完整的臨床技能視頻。它針對基於評分標準的過程級判斷，即互動驅動的狀態更新是否在整個工作流程中保持程序正確性。SiMing-Bench的實現依賴於SiMing-Score，這是一個由醫生註釋的真實臨床技能考試視頻數據集，涵蓋心肺復甦術、自動體外除顫器操作和袋面通氣，每個視頻都配有標準化的逐步評分標準和雙專家標籤。在各種開源和閉源的MLLM中，我們觀察到與醫生判斷的一致性始終較弱。此外，即使整體程序級相關性看起來可接受，對於基於評分標準的中間步驟的表現仍然較弱，這表明粗略的全球評估大幅高估了當前模型的程序判斷能力。進一步的分析顯示，二元步驟判斷和步驟對齊的片段表明，瓶頸不僅僅是細粒度評分或時間定位，而是建模持續互動如何隨時間更新程序狀態。
2604.09037.author: Xiyang Huang et.al.
2604.09037.authors: Xiyang Huang, Jiawei Lin, Keying Wu, Jiaxin Huang, Kailai Yang, Renxiong Wei, Cheng zeng, Jiayi Xiang, Ziyan Kuang, Min Peng, Qianqian Xie, Sophia Ananiadou
